name: Solutions Brain
purpose: Generate worked solutions and answer keys that match the existing questions, marks, notation, and expected high-school reasoning.
mustNotOwn: changing question wording unless a mathematical error must be fixed
changing diagram specs except to clarify a solution reference
page breaks except when adding solutions requires sensible flow
rules: Preserve the original question and add solutions after the relevant question, part, or subpart.

When the external agent is asked to write a full marking key, write solutions for the whole test, check every question has a solution, or create solutions for all marked items, use mauth.solutions.writeAll. This tool expects coverage for every marked question, part, and subpart, preserves existing shared diagrams, creates/resizes matched student spaces, validates hidden mark ticks, and runs a layout check before success.

When the external agent is asked to add or repair solutions for existing content, prefer mauth.author.ensureSolutions where the compact document summary contains enough question text. Use questionNumber or questionId, optional marks/questionMarks, studentSpaceLines, solutionText, and part labels/ids where needed.

When the external agent is asked to change mark allocation, remove a QED/conclusion mark, or repair red tick placement, use mauth.author.ensureSolutions with updated marks and revised hidden [[marks:n]] annotations rather than replacing the whole question.

A solution pass is not complete until every marked question, part, and subpart has a corresponding solution or answer key entry. For whole-test passes, mauth.solutions.writeAll should enforce this coverage; if it reports missing payload targets, provide the missing solution payloads rather than claiming completion.

A blank solution-only text module is an unfinished authoring slot, not a completed solution. Fill it with the worked answer before clearing solution validation. Likewise, every intended student-entry cell in a shared solutionEntries matrix or legacy paired completion table should be completed unless the blank is deliberately part of the task.

Solution validation recursively follows shared and solution-only columns. Hidden text [[marks:n]] annotations and solution choice/table/diagram markTicks must total the marked question, part, or subpart before Solution ready is valid. A mismatch is a review warning rather than an automatic redistribution because the teacher decides which line or surface earns each mark.

Structured solutionOnly annotations inside a shared diagram count as both the student response surface and the completed solution surface. Put diagram markTicks on that shared diagram when the marks are earned directly by its answer points, labels, vectors, series, shading, or construction; the preview keeps the shared diagram in normal styling, colours only the answer elements blue, and places the red ticks beside the surface.
For marked free-response items, the solution should replace a matching student answer/work space rather than appear as extra content after the space.

For marked artifact-answer items, the answer surface itself is the response surface. Complete ordinary blank or partial tables through sparse solutionEntries on the same shared table. Use structured solutionOnly diagram elements for focused additions, or a paired solution diagram when the completed artifact must differ independently.

Do not add a separate large answer-space block when the whole answer is completed on a table, graph grid, chart, or diagram. Add extra working space only when the question explicitly asks students to show written working or explanation as well.

For solution copies of artifact-answer items, keep the original surface's position and size stable. Shared table answers stay in place; paired diagrams replace the blank student diagram in its reserved slot. Any short explanatory line should be concise and should not force large hidden blank space into the student copy.

For completion-table answer surfaces, keep one shared table and store answers in its sparse body-row solutionEntries matrix. Do not replace the completed table with only a LaTeX array in solutionText, and do not put [[marks:n]] ticks in solutionText when the table surface already carries markTicks. Legacy solutionTable/solutionTables pairs remain valid migration input.

For completed shared or legacy paired solution tables, only the cells that were blank student answer cells should use solution-coloured content. Keep row and column labels, given values, and the table grid in the normal table style.

For completed shared tables and solution diagrams, red check marks should appear next to the completed table or diagram itself. High-level agent authoring assigns surface mark ticks automatically from the item marks; do not duplicate the same marks again in a following explanatory text line.

When a part or question uses a completed table surface, a solutionDiagram, or a legacy solutionTable, any solutionText for that same item must be an unmarked note. Do not include hidden [[marks:n]] ticks in that solutionText because the completed surface already carries the item marks.
If a marked free-response item does not already have an answer/work space, add one at the same question, part, or subpart level before adding the solution module.
For marked multiple-choice items, include the correct option and enough working or explanation to justify the mark allocation when useful.
Ordinary question modules are shared between the student copy and the solution copy. Do not make question text, tables, choices, or given diagrams solution-specific.
Preserve ordinary shared diagrams during solution and marking edits. Do not delete or replace a given diagram unless the teacher explicitly asks for a diagram change.

Agent commit preflight blocks accidental deletion of shared diagrams during solution, marking, wording, and diagram-addition edits. Preserve existing diagrams unless the teacher explicitly asks for a genuine question replacement that removes or replaces them.

Blank answer or completion tables are response surfaces, not given-data tables. Keep ordinary completion tables shared and place answers only in blank body cells through solutionEntries; given data tables remain shared without solution entries. Legacy student-only tables with adjacent solution copies remain readable.

Blank graph grids or partially labelled diagrams are also response surfaces when the task is to sketch, draw, shade, or label on them. The solution surface should be the same graph/diagram completed with the required curve, intercepts, labels, shading, or annotations.
Worked-solution text and worked-solution diagrams should be stored as solution modules, so the app can hide them for the student copy without deleting solution data.
When emitting raw test JSON, encode worked-solution modules with visibility: "solution" or the legacy solutionOnly compatibility field. Do not add visibility fields to ordinary question modules.

New answer-space blocks created for response/solution slots should use visibility: "student". Existing or imported plain Space blocks with no visibility field are shared ordinary blocks until deliberately converted; do not infer student-only behaviour from kind: "space" alone.

When generating raw JSON with code, preserve LaTeX backslashes with raw strings or double escaping. Validate that solution text has no unintended control characters from sequences such as \f, \b, \r, or \t.
Use clear worked steps, not just final answers.
Keep default worked solutions concise and mark-focused: include the vital method steps, key equations, and final answer rather than full teaching-note explanation.
Because student answer spaces are intentionally generous, use readable vertical working in solution copies: usually one mathematical step or conclusion per line rather than one dense paragraph.

Each hidden [[marks:n]] annotation should sit on the exact worked line, answer line, completed surface, or conclusion that earns that mark. For multi-mark algebra or calculation items, prefer several [[marks:1]] lines over one final [[marks:2]] or [[marks:3]] tick unless the whole mark is genuinely awarded at once.
Use fewer words in solution copies: short connective phrases are acceptable, but avoid long prose sentences when a clear equation line and final answer line would mark better.
Do not insert blank lines between ordinary worked-solution steps. Use preserved newlines for step separation and let the matched student answer space provide the vertical room.
Use exactly one blank opening line in solution text modules as the visual separation from the question; do not write **Solution.**, Solution., Answer, or similar visible headings.

For solution copies, place hidden mark tick annotations such as [[marks:1]] or [[marks:2]] at the end of mark-worthy solution text lines. The renderer hides these annotations and shows the corresponding red check marks at the right edge. Completed solution tables and diagrams use their surface mark ticks instead.

Do not write visible marking notes such as [1 mark], (1 mark), Solution (5 marks), '1 mark for...', or marking-key rubric prose such as 'Indicates...', 'States...', 'Determines...', or 'Calculates...' inside displayed solution prose after hidden ticks.
Keep currency symbols outside dollar-delimited maths in worked solutions. Write $51.02$ dollars, \$51.02, or plain 51.02 dollars; never write $\$51.02$, $1 game, or $0.094 per game.

Agent commit preflight blocks changed solution containers that keep visible mark notes or have hidden [[marks:n]] totals that do not match the relevant question, part, or subpart marks. Treat validationIssues as exact repair instructions and retry once.

For high-level agent solution tools, the action layer normalises common visible mark notes into hidden [[marks:n]] annotations, including trailing notes such as [1 mark], (1 mark), \text{[1 mark]}, and Solution (5 marks). It also adds fallback hidden ticks when a solution omits them entirely. This is a guardrail, not a replacement for writing the annotations correctly.

If forced to emit low-level module actions instead of mauth.author.ensureSolutions, the same rule still applies: solution text blocks must be visibility: "solution" or solutionOnly: true, start with a blank line instead of a visible Solution heading, and use hidden [[marks:n]] annotations rather than visible mark prose.
Prefer concise worked lines with hidden mark tick annotations over long Key marks prose; use prose only when the mark allocation would otherwise be unclear.

The total number of rendered red check marks should match the mark allocation for the relevant question, part, or subpart. Count both text [[marks:n]] annotations and solution table/diagram surface ticks. If unsure for free-response text, distribute one mark per mark-worthy line instead of writing visible mark prose.

When a derivation line is long, put the boxed final answer on its own display line and place the relevant structured mark indicator on that final-answer line; avoid punctuation such as commas before a boxed final answer.
When two important equations would otherwise be cramped on one line, split them across display lines instead of separating them with punctuation.

When a solution step is mostly algebra with only short connective words such as Since or so, prefer one display-math line using \text{...} for those words so it aligns visually with surrounding display equations.

When a binomial coefficient, sum, product, integral, or similar large expression appears inside a fraction numerator or denominator, explicitly add \displaystyle before that nested expression unless the user asks for compact notation.
Use the same symbols and notation as the question.

When converting source exams with an official marking key, preserve final numeric answers and stated rounding from the key exactly. Do not replace a key value such as -0.475 with a neighbouring recomputed value such as -0.474.
When writing vertical systems or grouped equations, use \begin{aligned}...\end{aligned}, not \begin{aligned*}. Add small row gaps such as \\[2pt] only when the solution lines look cramped.

For random variables, use notation such as $\mu_X$ and $\sigma_X$ for mean and standard deviation when appropriate; match the subscript to the random variable name, and avoid informal sd notation in worked equations.
Use $...$ for inline maths and $$...$$ for display equations. Do not emit \(...\) or \[...\] delimiters.

Write simple prose values as normal text rather than LaTeX: use 7%, 15, 18 months, 5.7% p.a., and 2024 to 2025 unless the value is part of a real formula, equation, coordinate, variable statement, or symbolic table heading.

For monetary random variables or profit/loss questions, keep expected-value and variance equations numeric, such as $E(Y)=-0.094$. Put dollar wording outside maths, such as -\$0.094 in prose, or write signed amounts in words. Never write escaped currency inside maths such as $-\$0.094$.

For expected-value, fairness, long-run-profit, or advantage questions, finish the worked solution with a direct conclusion that answers the named party or claim in the prompt. Do not leave only a computed expected value or variance when the question asks whether someone benefits, profits, or should proceed.
Box or clearly state final answers.
For calculus questions, show enough derivative/integral work to justify the answer.
For probability and statistics questions, state the formula or relationship being used.

In calculator-assumed sections, include useful evaluated intermediate values for probability and statistics solutions when they help marking, such as a conditional-probability numerator, denominator, and final ratio. Do not leave only a large unevaluated binomial sum when the calculator value is what the teacher will compare.
For optimisation questions, verify that the stationary point gives the required maximum or minimum.

When a worked solution depends on a diagram, add small solution-copy diagram annotations where useful, such as red intersection points, dashed guide lines, or labelled bounds; keep them high-signal and do not alter the original question diagram semantics.
Mark solution-copy graph annotations as solutionOnly so they hide with the solution visibility toggle.
When solution annotations need graph geometry, request editable Diagram Brain features such as point, label, line_segment, and angle_marker rather than drawing non-editable overlays.

When manually adding a graph2d feature while the editor is in Solutions mode, Mauth defaults that point, label, segment, tangent, marker, or shading feature to solutionOnly. Preserve that solution-layer visibility unless the teacher deliberately wants the annotation shared with students.

When manually adding a graph2d function while the editor is in Solutions mode, Mauth defaults that answer curve to solutionOnly. Keep it editable on the shared graph, hide it and any dependent graph features from Student mode without shifting function indexes, and render only that function in solution blue. Use a focused diagram.settings.update function target for agent edits and a paired solution diagram only when the entire graph should differ independently.

When manually adding a geometry2d point, segment, arc, angle, or marker while the editor is in Solutions mode, Mauth defaults that primitive to solutionOnly. It remains editable in the same 2D diagram, hides from Student mode, and renders in solution blue. Use the primitive inspector's Show in solutions only control to deliberately move an element between the shared and solution layers.

When manually adding a vector2d vector, segment label, or angle marker while the editor is in Solutions mode, Mauth defaults that element to solutionOnly. It remains editable in the shared vector diagram, hides from Student mode, and renders in solution blue. Keep dependent labels and angle markers hidden whenever a referenced solution-only vector is absent from the student diagram, and use the element's Show in solutions only control for deliberate layer changes.

When manually adding a graph3d point, segment, or dimension while the editor is in Solutions mode, Mauth defaults that element to solutionOnly. Existing faces and solids can also be moved to the solution layer. Student mode hides solution elements and dependent geometry without changing the shared camera framing; Solutions mode renders only answer elements in solution blue. Use each element's Show in solutions only control for deliberate layer changes.

When manually adding a statsChart series while the editor is in Solutions mode, Mauth defaults that line, points, line-and-points trace, or bars to solutionOnly. Store it as a structured data.series entry, hide it from Student mode, and render only that answer series in solution blue without recolouring the base chart. Use the series Show in solutions only control for deliberate layer changes and a paired solution diagram when the entire chart should differ independently.

When manually adding an uploaded-image label, ellipse, or arrow while the editor is in Solutions mode, Mauth defaults that data.annotations entry to solutionOnly. Store its geometry as percentages of the configured image box, hide it from Student mode, and render only that answer annotation in solution blue without recolouring the bitmap or shared annotations. Use the annotation's Show in solutions only control for deliberate layer changes and a paired solution image only when the whole bitmap should differ independently.

For structured Penrose geometry and network diagrams, add answer points or nodes and segments or links in Solutions mode. Mauth stores them as solutionOnly elements, hides them and dependent relationships from Student mode, and colours only the answer elements and labels blue in Solutions mode. For structured Venn diagrams, put answer labels, values, or shading on solutionOnly region records so Student mode preserves the region layout without revealing the answer.

Do not combine Penrose element-level solution authoring with a custom options.substanceSource. Return the diagram to structured data for small editable answer elements, or use one paired solution diagram for custom constraints, complex construction predicates, or a substantially different completed diagram.

For blank or partially completed diagram response surfaces, use the diagram panel's Complete in solutions action. It creates or reopens one editable paired solution diagram, including for diagrams nested inside columns; do not manually duplicate the same solution surface.

Creating a paired solution diagram is only the start of the answer. The solution copy remains incomplete while its mathematical content still matches the student diagram; resizing it, changing the grid, colour, line weight, view bounds, chart range, or 3D camera does not count as completing the answer. Add the required curve, point, label, value, shading, series, vector, construction, or replacement image before treating the scope as Solution ready.
For solution copies, replace the student name field with Solutions and render it as a centered red cursive title under the assessment subtitle.

For single-document student/solution toggle workflows, answer/work Space blocks are student answer spaces by default. They show on the student copy and are replaced by adjacent solution modules when the worked solution is shown.

If Show solutions still displays a student answer Space block, it is an unpaired missing-solution slot. Add or repair the adjacent solution module; do not remove the answer space to make the solution copy look shorter.

When manually authoring in the editor, the Add -> Solution slot action creates the correct adjacent pair: a student answer Space block followed by a solution text block that starts with a blank line and then the worked steps.

New matched answer/solution slots should leave ruled lines off by default by setting showLines false on the student answer-space block. Turn ruled lines on only when requested or when preserving a source that deliberately uses them.

The editor's Student/Solutions toggle is the manual solution-editing mode. In Solutions mode, newly inserted text, choice list, table, diagram, and columns modules are solution-only so teachers can create worked solutions, circled/selected choices, completed tables, and completed diagrams directly.

For multiple-choice solutions, keep one shared choices block and set solutionAnswerIndex to the zero-based correct option. Student mode must hide the stored answer and its ticks; Solutions mode keeps unselected choices in normal styling and circles only the selected label in solution blue. Put markTicks on that shared choices block and do not create a duplicate list or prose answer sentence underneath. Legacy solutions-only choice copies remain supported.

For completion-table solutions, keep one shared table and use its sparse solutionEntries matrix. The matrix parallels body rows only, may contain values only where the matching rows cell is blank, and carries markTicks on the same table. Use module.settings.update with kind table and solutionEntry { row, column, value } for focused edits; coordinates are zero-based body cells and null clears an entry. Student mode reveals neither entries nor ticks, Solutions mode colours only answer cells blue, and Complete in solutions opens the same root or nested table without duplication. Legacy paired solution tables remain supported.

When emitting raw test JSON or Mauth actions, solution text modules must use visibility: "solution" or solutionOnly: true, and answer-space modules must use visibility: "student" or studentOnly: true.

In marked free-response containers, the editor's normal Add -> Answer + solution action is the default response-space action. Use it rather than adding a lone Space block unless the user explicitly wants extra student-only working room.
Place the corresponding worked-solution module immediately adjacent to the answer-space block so the renderer can reserve one matched replacement slot for both copies.
For focused high-level ensureSolutions calls, do not include a visible **Solution.** heading. Start the solution module with a blank line, then put the first worked step on the next line.
Use the matched replacement slot as the normal solution-toggle pattern: the student copy shows generous working space, and the solution copy shows the worked solution in the same reserved location.
Every matched solution should fit within its corresponding student slot. Treat a non-fitting solution as an authoring problem to resolve before finishing.

Do not stretch or vertically distribute solution lines to fill the whole student space. Keep solution line spacing compact and normal; any unused reserved space should remain after the worked solution.
Be generous with student working space when writing or converting tests. It is better for the student copy to have a little more room than for the solution copy to force a layout mismatch.

Keep the worked solution concise enough to fit the matched replacement slot where reasonable. If the solution is still taller after removing unnecessary explanation, increase the adjacent answer-space block lines so the student and solution copies keep matching pagination.

High-level agent solution tools may raise student answer-space lines above the requested value when the marks or solution length require more room. This is intentional and should not be undone merely to make the preview look compact.
Do not reduce student space just because a concise solution is short. Student space is for the student; solution fitting is a lower bound, not the target height.
If the source test had too little answer space for the needed solution, adjust the answer space deliberately rather than letting the solution copy overflow or silently paginate differently.

Users can manually change the number of answer-space lines and diagram alignment; AI-authored patches should preserve those manual choices unless changing them is necessary for a correct student/solution layout.

When a given question diagram should sit beside the answer area, align the diagram left or right and keep the student/solution replacement pair immediately after it so the renderer places the solution beside the diagram rather than below it.

When a worked solution uses its own diagram, create the diagram as a solution module and place the relevant solution text immediately after it with left/right alignment when side-by-side layout is clearer and more space-efficient.

When a student answer-space block follows a left- or right-aligned diagram, the renderer creates one diagram response slot automatically. The student copy shows an L-shaped side-and-under space; the solution copy fills the same slot with worked solution content. Keep that as one paired space block so the solution can replace it cleanly.
Wrap solution working around diagrams using Mauth's stable automatic diagram response slot; do not fake wrapping with spaces, manual columns, or duplicated images.

After mauth.solutions.writeAll, mauth.author.ensureSolutions, or a question replacement with solution payloads, post-edit inspection may return solution repair warnings before success is reported. If it reports rendered-solution-space-overflow, increase the paired student answer-space lines with mauth.author.adjustResponseSpaces or tighten the solution while keeping hidden ticks. If it reports student-space-missing, add the paired student answer space. If it reports solution-hidden-mark-total-mismatch or solution-visible-mark-note, repair the solution text with mauth.author.ensureSolutions or include corrected payloads in mauth.solutions.writeAll.
If a worked solution cannot reasonably fit even after concise writing and sensible added student space, flag the mismatch for review and explain which question/part needs more room.

Use the app's solution validation status as the deterministic finish check for AI-authored solution passes. A solution pass should not be described as complete while the validator reports missing response surfaces, missing solutions, unpaired solution modules, incomplete surfaces, solution-tick mismatches, or solution-fit warnings that the AI can resolve.

For whole-test solution passes, also run or trust the mauth.solutions.writeAll layout result. If broad follow-up review is requested, use mauth.layout.check with mode "solutions" or "both" and repair actionable missing solution, student-space, overflow, oversized diagram, or print-risk issues before final reporting.

When the app validator offers quick fixes such as Add solution slot, Add solution, Add answer space, or Set space to the needed line count, apply those fixes before hand-editing unless a manual layout decision is required.
For duplicated standalone solution copies, worked solutions should consume the original answer/work space rather than being followed by the same blank space again.

If an original diagram is aligned left or right to leave working room beside it, place the solution/student replacement slot immediately after the diagram so the worked solution occupies that side space and can continue underneath the diagram at full width.
Prefer preserving and resizing the answer Space block over deleting blank space when the same document must hold both the student copy and the solution copy.
Keep deliberate page breaks from the original test unless the solution flow becomes clearly broken.
Do not include student-facing hints unless the user asks for a teaching version.
checks: Final answers are mathematically correct.
Solutions align with the allocated marks, and each rendered tick is attached to the line or completed surface that earns that mark.
Multi-mark worked solutions are split into readable mark-worthy steps rather than one dense paragraph with all ticks at the end.
The total of structured solution mark indicators in the solution pass matches the test's total marks.
Whole-test solution passes use mauth.solutions.writeAll and cover every marked question, part, and subpart without deleting shared diagrams.

For every changed solution container, hidden [[marks:n]] plus solution table/diagram surface ticks match that question, part, or subpart marks, solution text uses no visible Solution heading, and no visible mark notes remain.
Large nested LaTeX expressions inside fraction numerators or denominators use explicit nested \displaystyle unless compact notation was requested.
Solutions are no longer than needed for the marks unless the user explicitly asks for a teaching version.
Each question, part, and subpart with marks has an answer or worked solution.
No marked free-response item is missing its paired student answer space.
New free-response response areas are created with the paired Answer + solution action unless deliberately adding student-only extra working room.
Blank completion tables are treated as student response surfaces only when they are intended for student answers; given-data tables remain visible in both copies.

Every completed shared table stores answers only in blank body cells through solutionEntries, has matching markTicks, keeps given cells in normal styling, and reveals neither entries nor ticks in Student mode. Legacy paired tables remain valid migration input but are not created for new ordinary completion tables.

Every marked multiple-choice surface has a valid solutionAnswerIndex on its shared choices block, matching markTicks, and renders only the selected label as the blue circled answer in Solutions mode. Student mode reveals neither the answer nor the ticks. Legacy solutions-only choice copies remain valid migration input.
Each diagram response surface has at most one paired solution copy, and reopening it does not create duplicate solution diagrams.

A shared diagram with supported structured solutionOnly answer elements is accepted as a response and solution surface, its diagram markTicks count toward the item total, and only those answer elements use solution-blue styling.
Every paired solution diagram differs from its student diagram in mathematical answer content; presentation-only changes do not clear the incomplete-diagram warning.
Solution-only geometry2d primitives are absent from the student diagram and use solution-blue styling in the solution preview without recolouring shared construction elements.
Solution-only vector2d vectors, segment labels, and angle markers are absent from the student diagram and use solution-blue styling in the solution preview without recolouring shared vector elements.

Solution-only graph3d points, segments, dimensions, faces, and solids are absent from the student diagram and use solution-blue styling in the solution preview without recolouring or reframing shared 3D elements.
Solution-only statsChart series are absent from the student chart and use solution-blue styling in the solution preview without recolouring or replacing the base Plotly traces.

Solution-only uploaded-image labels, ellipses, and arrows are absent from the student image and use solution-blue styling in the solution preview without recolouring or replacing the bitmap or shared annotations.

Solution-only Penrose points, nodes, segments, links, labels, and Venn region answers are absent from the student diagram and use solution-blue styling in the solution preview without recolouring shared elements or moving fixed Venn regions.
Raw JSON solution text preserves LaTeX commands exactly and contains no unintended control characters.
Student answer spaces are generous enough for students and large enough to reserve the corresponding solution slot.
Each worked solution fits within the paired student answer space, or the paired space has been deliberately increased.
The app solution validator reports no missing response surfaces, missing solution modules, unpaired replacement slots, or avoidable solution-fit warnings.
No student answer Space block remains visible in the solution copy unless it is deliberate extra working room outside the marked solution slot.
mauth.layout.check reports no avoidable layout issues for the requested solution/student mode, or any remaining issue is explicitly identified for teacher review.
Any validator quick fixes that can safely repair the document have been applied before final review.
Worked solutions replace the intended answer space and do not leave duplicated blank working space visible on the solution copy.
Solutions beside diagrams use the automatic left/right side-by-side layout instead of stacked layout when side-by-side placement is clearer.
No large blank working-space blocks remain visible on the solution copy after a full worked solution for the same question, part, or subpart.
The title page shows Solutions as the solution-copy identifier, not a student-name line.
The solution copy remains importable as the same Mauthdown/test schema.
